evals.name: full-review-test-old-cluster
evals.prompt: Run a full operational excellence assessment on the EKS cluster currently configured in my kubeconfig. Cover all 10 sections and produce the rated report.
evals.expected_output: A markdown report with executive summary, maturity score, 10 sections of findings with consistent ratings, prioritized actions ordered by severity, items to investigate manually, and AWS reference links sourced from the pre-verified reference map.
expectations: The report contains all required top-level sections in this order: Executive Summary, Maturity Score, Findings (with all 10 numbered sections present), Prioritized Actions (Critical, Important, Quick Wins), Items to Investigate Manually, AWS Reference Links. TODO: human review

Every finding rated RED appears in the Critical actions table, and every finding rated AMBER appears in either Important or Quick Wins — no RED/AMBER finding is missing from Prioritized Actions. TODO: human review

Ratings are consistent across the report: a finding rated RED in the Findings table is also called out as RED in the Executive Summary and named with its finding ID (e.g., '4.1 — Control Plane Logging RED') in Prioritized Actions. TODO: human review
Critical actions are ordered by blast radius (security > availability > cost) — security-flagged RED findings appear before availability- or cost-flagged ones. TODO: human review

Every RED finding has a specific, actionable recommendation (not boilerplate like 'enable logging'); UNKNOWN items are explained in 'Items to Investigate Manually' with a question the user can answer. TODO: human review
All AWS reference URLs come from the pre-verified reference map in `references/report-generation.md` Step 7 — no fabricated deep-link URLs. TODO: human review

knowledge_assertions.context: Report must include an Executive Summary section
Report must include a Maturity Score section
Report must include Prioritized Actions section with Critical/Important/Quick Wins
Findings must use the standard rating vocabulary
Section 01 (Cluster Lifecycle & Upgrades) must be present in a full review
Section 03 (Access & Identity) must be present in a full review
Section 04 (Observability) must be present in a full review
Section 06 (Networking) must be present in a full review
Section 07 (Autoscaling) must be present in a full review
Section 10 (Add-on Management) must be present in a full review
Report must include AWS reference links from the pre-verified reference map

knowledge_assertions.pattern: sample code for educational and demonstration purposes only
knowledge_assertions.context: Report must include the sample-code disclaimer footer
Must not recommend deprecated kube2iam for IAM

evals.name: section-scoped-networking-review
evals.prompt: Run a networking-scoped operational review on the EKS cluster in my current kubeconfig context.
evals.expected_output: A focused report containing only the networking section findings, with consistent ratings, prioritized actions for any networking RED/AMBER findings, and AWS reference links scoped to networking topics.
expectations: The report focuses on Section 06 (Networking) only — does NOT include findings from other sections like Cluster Lifecycle, Access & Identity, etc. TODO: human review
The skill loaded `references/networking.md` as the source for checks (visible from the assessment narrative or the report's reference links section). TODO: human review

Networking findings are rated only on observed data: subnet IP capacity is checked against actual subnets, CoreDNS health against actual pod state, NetworkPolicy presence against actual cluster resources. TODO: human review

If any networking check fails (e.g., MCP unable to list resources, missing permissions), the corresponding item is marked UNKNOWN with a specific reason — not silently skipped or guessed. TODO: human review

knowledge_assertions.context: Report must cover the Networking section
Findings must use the standard rating vocabulary
Networking review must assess VPC subnet IP capacity
Networking review must assess CoreDNS health
Networking review must check for NetworkPolicy presence
Networking review must reference VPC CNI as the networking plugin
Networking review should check for prefix delegation configuration
Section-scoped review must NOT include other sections like Cluster Lifecycle
Report must include AWS reference links for networking topics
